narratives.about:
Abbeville is a community in Louisiana (LA), whose population declined roughly 11% over the past ten years. The city has a population density of 1,820 inhabitants per square mile. Home to approximately 11,003 inhabitants, it reflects the economic and social currents shaping urban America.

Economically, Abbeville shows a median household income of $33,015 (below-average). Unemployment sits at 3.8%, near the national unemployment benchmark. The average weekly wage is $1,078. Employment growth is running at +16% year-over-year. Roughly 32.4% of residents fall below the federal poverty line.

The housing market in Abbeville sits more affordable than the national norm. At $97,300 (2.9× income, relatively accessible homeownership), home purchases require careful financial planning. HUD's Fair Market Rent for a 2-bedroom unit is $1,019/month. The homeownership rate is 49.6%. Renters typically pay $829/month.

On public safety, FBI data places Abbeville in the category of significantly elevated violent crime. Violent crime is 775 per 100,000 community members vs. a U.S. average of 274.7. Property crime stands at 2,133.5 per 100k (above average). The murder rate of 18.2 per 100k is a concern warranting targeted policy attention. Neighborhood-level data varies and should be reviewed when evaluating specific areas.

CDC PLACES health data for Abbeville shows: adult obesity stands at 48.2% (above average); diabetes affects 17.6% of adults; 25% of adults smoke (above average). 12.7% lack health insurance, a key access barrier. 60.8% of the population is enrolled in Medicare.

Environmental quality in Abbeville: EPA reports a median Air Quality Index of 51 (Moderate — acceptable for most) and PM2.5 concentration of 8.3 µg/m³. According to NOAA, the area sees the annual average temperature of 68.5°F, annual precipitation of 53 inches.

Abbeville's broader profile reflects how economic, social, and environmental indicators interact over time. Data on this page is sourced from: U.S. Census Bureau (ACS), Bureau of Labor Statistics, FBI, Census Housing Data, HUD Fair Market Rents, CDC PLACES, EPA, NOAA, USDA ERS.

Livability fit: Abbeville is best suited for young professionals building early-career financial flexibility, based on the current balance of safety, cost pressure, and local economic conditions.

Pros: affordable housing baseline; resilient local labor market. Cons: elevated poverty pressure in some neighborhoods.

Migration signal: current data suggests steady, moderate appeal rather than a high-velocity migration surge.

How this page is built: Abbeville data is assembled from official U.S. public sources, then normalized for comparability where possible. Because agencies use different boundaries and reporting lags, this narrative emphasizes directional interpretation and uncertainty rather than deterministic conclusions.

faq.q: Is Abbeville, Louisiana (LA) a good place to live?
faq.a: Abbeville offers mixed, with some challenges to consider.  Key considerations: elevated crime rates, elevated poverty rates. As with any city, the best fit depends on individual priorities such as career field, family needs, and lifestyle preferences.

faq.q: What is the population of Abbeville?
faq.a: Abbeville has a population of approximately 11,003 residents according to the most recent available data. Over the past decade, the city's population changed by -10.8%.

faq.q: What is the crime rate in Abbeville?
faq.a: Abbeville records a violent crime rate of 775 incidents per 100,000 residents. This is significantly above the national average of 274.7. Crime statistics are self-reported by local agencies to the FBI and may not capture all incidents.

faq.q: How affordable is housing in Abbeville?
faq.a: Housing in Abbeville is reasonably affordable compared to many U.S. metros. The median home value is $97,300, roughly 2.9× the median household income.  Affordability varies significantly by neighborhood.

faq.q: What is the median household income in Abbeville?
faq.a: The median household income in Abbeville is $33,015 per year. This is below the national average, which may reflect local economic challenges or cost-of-living trade-offs. Income data is sourced from the U.S. Census Bureau's American Community Survey.

faq.q: What is the unemployment rate in Abbeville?
faq.a: The unemployment rate in Abbeville is currently 3.8%, which is broadly in line with the national average. Unemployment data is reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

faq.q: How are the statistics on this Abbeville page calculated?
faq.a: Most values use the latest available official release for each metric, with trend context from historical yearly records when available. Some indicators are city-specific, while others are county or metro-area proxies depending on the source agency. Comparisons on this page are descriptive and should not be interpreted as causal proof or personalized advice.

faq.q: Why can different indicators on Abbeville refer to different years?
faq.a: Federal agencies publish on different schedules, so the most recent valid year can vary by metric. This page keeps each indicator at its latest reliable release instead of forcing all metrics into a single year, which preserves data accuracy but may reduce direct year-to-year comparability across categories.

narratives.snapshot: Abbeville, Louisiana (LA) is a shrinking urban area with approximately 11,003 residents. With a median household income of $33,015, Abbeville features below-average household incomes. On the housing front, median home prices are $97,300, below the national benchmark. The local economy shows unemployment in line with national norms (3.8%). Public safety data indicates elevated crime rates that merit attention. Overall, this snapshot outlines where Abbeville currently stands across core affordability, income, and public-safety signals. Snapshot tags: Affordable.
